input.command: cd /mydata/karma/runs/short-rerun/elasticsearch-drift-monitoring-seed-20260618_102231/stages/stage_02/; echo "===prompt==="; cat prompt.txt; echo "===precondition==="; cat precondition.log

content.content:
===prompt===
Stack monitoring data is not showing up in the monitoring cluster.

OBSERVED STATE:
- Elasticsearch is running in the elasticsearch namespace.
- Monitoring cluster is running in the monitoring namespace.

YOUR TASK:
Restore monitoring data flow so the monitoring cluster receives Elasticsearch
monitoring data. Stack monitoring covers both a metrics path and a logs path,
so every Elasticsearch pod must run BOTH a Metricbeat (metrics) and a Filebeat
(logs) monitoring sidecar that ship to the monitoring cluster.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do not install CRDs or use operators.
- Keep the Elasticsearch cluster healthy.

GOAL:
- Monitoring indices appear in the monitoring cluster.
- Every Elasticsearch pod runs both a metricbeat and a filebeat monitoring sidecar.===precondition===
[precondition:es_env_ready:preconditionUnits[0] 'es_env_ready':probe] $ kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running
5

[precondition:monitoring_env_ready:preconditionUnits[1] 'monitoring_env_ready':probe] $ kubectl -n monitoring get pods -l app=monitoring-es 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running
0

[precondition:monitoring_env_ready:preconditionUnits[1] 'monitoring_env_ready':apply] $ kubectl delete namespace monitoring --ignore-not-found=true --wait=false

[precondition:monitoring_env_ready:preconditionUnits[1] 'monitoring_env_ready':apply] $ kubectl create namespace monitoring
namespace/monitoring created

[precondition:monitoring_env_ready:preconditionUnits[1] 'monitoring_env_ready':apply] $ kubectl -n monitoring apply -f cases/elasticsearch/stack-monitoring-sidecars/resource/monitoring-cluster/
pod/monitoring-curl-test created
deployment.apps/monitoring-es created
service/monitoring-es-http created

[precondition:monitoring_env_ready:preconditionUnits[1] 'monitoring_env_ready':apply] $ kubectl -n monitoring wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=monitoring-es --timeout=300s
pod/monitoring-es-58c4994b7f-7js9v condition met

[precondition:monitoring_env_ready:preconditionUnits[1] 'monitoring_env_ready':apply] $ kubectl -n monitoring wait --for=condition=ready pod/monitoring-curl-test --timeout=300s
pod/monitoring-curl-test condition met

[precondition:monitoring_env_ready:preconditionUnits[1] 'monitoring_env_ready':verify] $ kubectl -n monitoring get pods 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running
2

  name: es
  namespace: elasticsearch

spec:
  persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy:
    whenDeleted: Retain
    whenScaled: Retain
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  replicas: 3
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  serviceName: es-discovery
  template:
    metadata:
      annotations:
        kubectl.kubernetes.io/restartedAt: "2026-06-18T05:44:37-05:00"
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      affinity:
        podAntiAffinity:
          preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
          - podAffinityTerm:
              labelSelector:
                matchLabels:
                  app: elasticsearch
              topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname
            weight: 100
      containers:
      - env:
        - name: node.name
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
          value: -Xms512m -Xmx512m
        - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: password
              name: elastic-password
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: elasticsearch
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9200
          name: https
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 9300
          name: transport
          protocol: TCP
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - bash
            - -c
            - |
              curl -s -k -u "elastic:$ELASTIC_PASSWORD" "https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health?wait_for_status=yellow&timeout=5s" | grep -q -E '"status":"(green|yellow)"'
          failureThreshold: 30
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 10
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: 1Gi
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
          name: data
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
          name: config
          subPath: elasticsearch.yml
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
          name: certs
          readOnly: true
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      initContainers:
      - command:
        - sh
        - -c
        - sysctl -w vm.max_map_count=262144 || true
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: sysctl
        resources: {}
        securityContext:
          privileged: true
          runAsUser: 0
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
      volumes:
      - configMap:
          defaultMode: 420
          name: es-config
        name: config
      - name: certs
        secret:
          defaultMode: 420
          secretName: es-http-tls
  updateStrategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      partition: 0
    type: RollingUpdate
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - apiVersion: v1
    kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      name: data
    spec:
      accessModes:
      - ReadWriteOnce
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 2Gi
      volumeMode: Filesystem
    status:
      phase: Pending

content.content:
=====filebeat-config.yaml=====
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: filebeat-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  filebeat.yml: |
    filebeat.inputs:
      - type: log
        enabled: true
        paths:
          - /var/log/es/*.log

    output.elasticsearch:
      hosts: ["http://monitoring-es-http.monitoring.svc:9200"]

    logging.to_files: false
    logging.to_syslog: false
=====metricbeat-config.yaml=====
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: metricbeat-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  metricbeat.yml: |
    metricbeat.modules:
      - module: elasticsearch
        xpack.enabled: true
        period: 10s
        hosts: ["https://localhost:9200"]
        username: elastic
        password: ${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}
        ssl.certificate_authorities: ["/etc/es-http/ca.crt"]

    output.elasticsearch:
      hosts: ["http://monitoring-es-http.monitoring.svc:9200"]

    logging.to_files: false
    logging.to_syslog: false
=====configmap.yaml=====
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${POD_NAME}
    node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - es-cluster-0.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - es-cluster-1.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - es-cluster-2.es-cluster.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - es-cluster-0
      - es-cluster-1
      - es-cluster-2
    node.store.allow_mmap: false
    path.logs: /var/log/es
    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: ["/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/ca.crt"]
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: ["/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/http-certs/ca.crt"]
=====passwords.yaml=====
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: elastic-password
  namespace: elasticsearch
type: Opaque
stringData:
  password: elasticpass
=====services.yaml=====
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-cluster
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: es-cluster
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: es-cluster
  ports:
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
      targetPort: 9300
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: es-cluster
spec:
  selector:
    app: es-cluster
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200

input.command: cat /mydata/karma/cases/elasticsearch/stack-monitoring-sidecars/oracle/oracle.py

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys

MON_NS = "monitoring"
ES_NS = "elasticsearch"
MON_SERVICE = "monitoring-es-http"
MON_CURL = "monitoring-curl-test"
# Hint for the Elasticsearch pod app label. Used as an override when it matches a
# live StatefulSet's selector; otherwise the real selector label is detected
# live from the cluster. The env PERSISTS across stages, so a workflow's
# inherited ES cluster may label its pods differently than this case's standalone
# default of 'es-cluster'.
ES_APP_HINT = os.environ.get("BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX", "es-cluster")
DEFAULT_SCHEME = "http"
_SCHEME = {}


def run(cmd):
    return subprocess.run(cmd, text=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE)


_ES_APP_LABEL = None


def resolve_es_app_label():
    """Resolve the 'app=<value>' selector for the live ES pods in ES_NS.

    Priority: the BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX hint when some live StatefulSet
    actually selects on app=<hint> (explicit override wins) -> the app label of
    the namespace's Elasticsearch StatefulSet detected live -> the hint.
    Workflow-agnostic: adapts to an inherited cluster labelled e.g.
    app=elasticsearch instead of app=es-cluster.
    """
    global _ES_APP_LABEL
    if _ES_APP_LABEL is not None:
        return _ES_APP_LABEL
    res = run(["kubectl", "-n", ES_NS, "get", "sts", "-o", "json"])
    labels = []
    if res.returncode == 0:
        try:
            for sts in json.loads(res.stdout).get("items", []):
                spec = sts.get("spec", {})
                containers = spec.get("template", {}).get("spec", {}).get("containers", [])
                if "elasticsearch" not in " ".join(c.get("image", "") for c in containers):
                    continue
                app = (spec.get("selector", {}).get("matchLabels", {}) or {}).get("app")
                ts = sts.get("metadata", {}).get("creationTimestamp", "")
                if app:
                    labels.append((app, ts))
        except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError):
            pass
    if any(app == ES_APP_HINT for app, _ in labels):
        _ES_APP_LABEL = f"app={ES_APP_HINT}"
        return _ES_APP_LABEL
    if labels:
        labels.sort(key=lambda x: (x[1] or ""))
        _ES_APP_LABEL = f"app={labels[0][0]}"
        return _ES_APP_LABEL
    _ES_APP_LABEL = f"app={ES_APP_HINT}"
    return _ES_APP_LABEL


def _probe_scheme(service, scheme):
    """True if the monitoring ES HTTP API answers on the given scheme.

    The env PERSISTS across stages, so the monitoring cluster's live scheme may
    differ from the default; a 401 still proves the scheme is reachable.
    """
    result = run([
        "kubectl", "-n", MON_NS, "exec", MON_CURL, "--",
        "curl", "-s", "-S", "-k", "-o", "/dev/null",
        "-w", "%{http_code}", "--max-time", "5",
        f"{scheme}://{service}.{MON_NS}.svc:9200/",
    ])
    code = (result.stdout or "").strip()
    return result.returncode == 0 and code.isdigit() and code != "000"


def detect_scheme(service):
    """Detect a service's live HTTP scheme (http default first, then https)."""
    if service in _SCHEME:
        return _SCHEME[service]
    for scheme in (DEFAULT_SCHEME, "https" if DEFAULT_SCHEME == "http" else "http"):
        if _probe_scheme(service, scheme):
            _SCHEME[service] = scheme
            return scheme
    _SCHEME[service] = DEFAULT_SCHEME
    return DEFAULT_SCHEME


def curl_json(service, path, errors):
    scheme = detect_scheme(service)
    result = run(
        [
            "kubectl",
            "-n",
            MON_NS,
            "exec",
            MON_CURL,
            "--",
            "curl",
            "-s",
            "-S",
            "-k",
            "--max-time",
            "10",
            f"{scheme}://{service}.{MON_NS}.svc:9200{path}",
        ]
    )
    if result.returncode != 0:
        detail = result.stderr.strip() or result.stdout.strip() or f"exit {result.returncode}"
        errors.append(f"Failed to query monitoring cluster {path}: {detail}")
        return None
    output = result.stdout.strip()
    if not output:
        errors.append(f"Empty response for monitoring {path}")
        return None
    try:
        return json.loads(output)
    except json.JSONDecodeError:
        errors.append(f"Failed to parse JSON from monitoring {path}")
        return None


def check_sidecars(errors):
    result = run(
        [
            "kubectl",
            "-n",
            ES_NS,
            "get",
            "pods",
            "-l",
            resolve_es_app_label(),
            "-o",
            "json",
        ]
    )
    if result.returncode != 0:
        detail = result.stderr.strip() or result.stdout.strip() or f"exit {result.returncode}"
        errors.append(f"Failed to list Elasticsearch pods: {detail}")
        return
    pods = json.loads(result.stdout).get("items", [])
    if not pods:
        errors.append("No Elasticsearch pods found")
        return

    for pod in pods:
        names = {c.get("name") for c in pod.get("spec", {}).get("containers", [])}
        if "metricbeat" not in names or "filebeat" not in names:
            errors.append("Missing metricbeat or filebeat sidecar in Elasticsearch pods")
            return


def check_monitoring_indices(errors):
    indices = curl_json(MON_SERVICE, "/_cat/indices?format=json", errors)
    if not isinstance(indices, list):
        return
    monitoring = [i for i in indices if i.get("index", "").startswith(".monitoring-es")]
    if not monitoring:
        errors.append("Monitoring indices not found in monitoring cluster")
        return

    # Ensure at least one monitoring index has documents.
    for index in monitoring:
        count = curl_json(MON_SERVICE, f"/{index['index']}/_count", errors)
        if isinstance(count, dict) and isinstance(count.get("count"), int):
            if count["count"] > 0:
                return
    errors.append("Monitoring indices exist but have no documents")


def main():
    errors = []

    check_sidecars(errors)
    check_monitoring_indices(errors)

    if errors:
        print("Stack monitoring sidecars verification failed:", file=sys.stderr)
        for err in errors:
            print(f"  - {err}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1

    print("Stack monitoring sidecars verified")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys

MON_NS = "monitoring"
ES_NS = "elasticsearch"
MON_SERVICE = "monitoring-es-http"
MON_CURL = "monitoring-curl-test"
# Hint for the Elasticsearch pod app label. Used as an override when it matches a
# live StatefulSet's selector; otherwise the real selector label is detected
# live from the cluster. The env PERSISTS across stages, so a workflow's
# inherited ES cluster may label its pods differently than this case's standalone
# default of 'es-cluster'.
ES_APP_HINT = os.environ.get("BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX", "es-cluster")
DEFAULT_SCHEME = "http"
_SCHEME = {}


def run(cmd):
    return subprocess.run(cmd, text=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE)


_ES_APP_LABEL = None


def resolve_es_app_label():

    Priority: the BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX hint when some live StatefulSet
    actually selects on app=<hint> (explicit override wins) -> the app label of
    the namespace's Elasticsearch StatefulSet detected live -> the hint.
    Workflow-agnostic: adapts to an inherited cluster labelled e.g.
    app=elasticsearch instead of app=es-cluster.
    """

        return _ES_APP_LABEL
    res = run(["kubectl", "-n", ES_NS, "get", "sts", "-o", "json"])
    labels = []
    if res.returncode == 0:
        try:
            for sts in json.loads(res.stdout).get("items", []):
                spec = sts.get("spec", {})
                containers = spec.get("template", {}).get("spec", {}).get("containers", [])
                if "elasticsearch" not in " ".join(c.get("image", "") for c in containers):
                    continue
                app = (spec.get("selector", {}).get("matchLabels", {}) or {}).get("app")
                ts = sts.get("metadata", {}).get("creationTimestamp", "")
                if app:
                    labels.append((app, ts))
        except (json.JSONDecodeError, AttributeError):
            pass
    if any(app == ES_APP_HINT for app, _ in labels):
        _ES_APP_LABEL = f"app={ES_APP_HINT}"
        return _ES_APP_LABEL
    if labels:
        labels.sort(key=lambda x: (x[1] or ""))
        _ES_APP_LABEL = f"app={labels[0][0]}"
        return _ES_APP_LABEL
    _ES_APP_LABEL = f"app={ES_APP_HINT}"
    return _ES_APP_LABEL

    """True if the monitoring ES HTTP API answers on the given scheme.

    The env PERSISTS across stages, so the monitoring cluster's live scheme may
    differ from the default; a 401 still proves the scheme is reachable.

        "kubectl", "-n", MON_NS, "exec", MON_CURL, "--",
        "curl", "-s", "-S", "-k", "-o", "/dev/null",
        "-w", "%{http_code}", "--max-time", "5",
        f"{scheme}://{service}.{MON_NS}.svc:9200/",
    ])
    code = (result.stdout or "").strip()
    return result.returncode == 0 and code.isdigit() and code != "000"


def detect_scheme(service):
    """Detect a service's live HTTP scheme (http default first, then https)."""
    if service in _SCHEME:
        return _SCHEME[service]
    for scheme in (DEFAULT_SCHEME, "https" if DEFAULT_SCHEME == "http" else "http"):
        if _probe_scheme(service, scheme):
            _SCHEME[service] = scheme
            return scheme
    _SCHEME[service] = DEFAULT_SCHEME
    return DEFAULT_SCHEME


def curl_json(service, path, errors):
    scheme = detect_scheme(service)
    result = run(
        [
            "kubectl",
            "-n",
            MON_NS,
            "exec",
            MON_CURL,
            "--",
            "curl",
            "-s",
            "-S",
            "-k",
            "--max-time",
            "10",
            f"{scheme}://{service}.{MON_NS}.svc:9200{path}",
        ]
    )
    if result.returncode != 0:
        detail = result.stderr.strip() or result.stdout.strip() or f"exit {result.returncode}"
        errors.append(f"Failed to query monitoring cluster {path}: {detail}")
        return None
    output = result.stdout.strip()
    if not output:
        errors.append(f"Empty response for monitoring {path}")
        return None
    try:
        return json.loads(output)
    except json.JSONDecodeError:
        errors.append(f"Failed to parse JSON from monitoring {path}")
        return None


def check_sidecars(errors):
    result = run(
        [
            "kubectl",
            "-n",
            ES_NS,
            "get",
            "pods",
            "-l",
            resolve_es_app_label(),
            "-o",
            "json",
        ]
    )
    if result.returncode != 0:
        detail = result.stderr.strip() or result.stdout.strip() or f"exit {result.returncode}"
        errors.append(f"Failed to list Elasticsearch pods: {detail}")
        return
    pods = json.loads(result.stdout).get("items", [])
    if not pods:
        errors.append("No Elasticsearch pods found")
        return

    for pod in pods:
        names = {c.get("name") for c in pod.get("spec", {}).get("containers", [])}
        if "metricbeat" not in names or "filebeat" not in names:
            errors.append("Missing metricbeat or filebeat sidecar in Elasticsearch pods")
            return


def check_monitoring_indices(errors):
    indices = curl_json(MON_SERVICE, "/_cat/indices?format=json", errors)
    if not isinstance(indices, list):
        return

    if not monitoring:
        errors.append("Monitoring indices not found in monitoring cluster")
        return

    # Ensure at least one monitoring index has documents.
    for index in monitoring:
        count = curl_json(MON_SERVICE, f"/{index['index']}/_count", errors)
        if isinstance(count, dict) and isinstance(count.get("count"), int):
            if count["count"] > 0:
                return
    errors.append("Monitoring indices exist but have no documents")


def main():
    errors = []

    check_sidecars(errors)
    check_monitoring_indices(errors)

    if errors:
        print("Stack monitoring sidecars verification failed:", file=sys.stderr)
        for err in errors:
            print(f"  - {err}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1

    print("Stack monitoring sidecars verified")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

content.text: The live ES uses `app=elasticsearch` label and default log path. Now I'll create the sidecar configs and patch the StatefulSet. Let me apply the provided metricbeat config and create a filebeat config matching the real log path.

input.command:
kubectl -n elasticsearch apply -f /mydata/karma/cases/elasticsearch/stack-monitoring-sidecars/resource/metricbeat-config.yaml
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl -n elasticsearch apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: filebeat-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  filebeat.yml: |
    filebeat.inputs:
      - type: log
        enabled: true
        paths:
          - /usr/share/elasticsearch/logs/*.json
    output.elasticsearch:
      hosts: ["http://monitoring-es-http.monitoring.svc:9200"]
    setup.ilm.enabled: false
    setup.template.enabled: false
    logging.to_files: false
    logging.to_syslog: false
EOF

content.content:
configmap/metricbeat-config created
configmap/filebeat-config created

tool_use_result.stdout:
configmap/metricbeat-config created
configmap/filebeat-config created

content.text: Now I'll patch the StatefulSet to add a shared logs volume into the ES container plus the two sidecars.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/sts-patch.yaml <<'EOF'
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      volumes:
        - name: es-logs
          emptyDir: {}
        - name: metricbeat-config
          configMap:
            name: metricbeat-config
        - name: filebeat-config
          configMap:
            name: filebeat-config
        - name: es-http-certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          volumeMounts:
            - name: es-logs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/logs
        - name: metricbeat
          image: docker.elastic.co/beats/metricbeat:8.11.1
          args: ["-e", "-c", "/etc/metricbeat/metricbeat.yml"]
          env:
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
          volumeMounts:
            - name: metricbeat-config
              mountPath: /etc/metricbeat/metricbeat.yml
              subPath: metricbeat.yml
            - name: es-http-certs
              mountPath: /etc/es-http
              readOnly: true
        - name: filebeat
          image: docker.elastic.co/beats/filebeat:8.11.1
          args: ["-e", "-c", "/etc/filebeat/filebeat.yml"]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: filebeat-config
              mountPath: /etc/filebeat/filebeat.yml
              subPath: filebeat.yml
            - name: es-logs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/logs
              readOnly: true
EOF
kubectl -n elasticsearch patch statefulset es --type=strategic --patch-file /tmp/sts-patch.yaml

input.command:
echo "=== log files in es-0 ==="; kubectl -n elasticsearch exec es-0 -c filebeat -- ls /usr/share/elasticsearch/logs/ 2>/dev/null
echo "=== filebeat harvester/output stats es-0 ==="; kubectl -n elasticsearch logs es-0 -c filebeat --tail=30 2>&1 | grep -oE '"harvester":\{[^}]*\}|"output":\{"events":\{[^}]*\}' | tail -5
input.description: Check es log files and filebeat harvesting

content.text: Only `gc.log` files exist (ES uses console logging for server logs, so no `.json` files). Let me broaden filebeat to harvest the `.log` files so the logs path actually ships data, then restart filebeat.

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl -n elasticsearch apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: filebeat-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  filebeat.yml: |
    filebeat.inputs:
      - type: log
        enabled: true
        paths:
          - /usr/share/elasticsearch/logs/*.log
          - /usr/share/elasticsearch/logs/*.json
    output.elasticsearch:
      hosts: ["http://monitoring-es-http.monitoring.svc:9200"]
    setup.ilm.enabled: false
    setup.template.enabled: false
    logging.to_files: false
    logging.to_syslog: false
EOF
kubectl -n elasticsearch rollout restart sts/es

content.content:
configmap/filebeat-config configured
statefulset.apps/es restarted

tool_use_result.stdout:
configmap/filebeat-config configured
statefulset.apps/es restarted
